words.meaning_en: A close connection or relationship between people
words.example_sentence: Years of shared experiences created a strong bond between them.
words.fill_blank: A true friendship creates a ______ that lasts a lifetime.

words.meaning_en: To restore friendly relations after a disagreement
words.example_sentence: After the argument, the two friends decided to reconcile.
words.fill_blank: It takes courage to ______ after a serious fight.

words.meaning_en: The act of breaking trust or faith with someone
words.example_sentence: She felt deep hurt at her best friend's betrayal.
words.fill_blank: Nothing wounds more deeply than a close friend's ______.

words.meaning_en: A person who spends time with or travels with another
words.example_sentence: His dog was his constant companion on long walks.
words.fill_blank: She was a loyal ______ throughout the difficult journey.

words.meaning_en: Able to be relied on as honest and faithful
words.example_sentence: A trustworthy friend keeps your secrets safe.
words.fill_blank: Choose ______ people to share your personal problems with.

words.meaning_en: Warm and friendly in manner
words.example_sentence: Despite their past rivalry, the two teams had a cordial meeting.
words.fill_blank: They maintained a ______ relationship even after the argument.

words.meaning_en: A person one knows slightly but not closely
words.example_sentence: He is an acquaintance, not a close friend.
words.fill_blank: She had many ______ at work but few real friends.

words.meaning_en: Competition or opposition between people for the same goal
words.example_sentence: The friendly rivalry between the two students pushed both to excel.
words.fill_blank: A healthy ______ encourages both sides to improve.

words.meaning_en: Genuinely meaning what one says or does; not pretending
words.example_sentence: A sincere friend always tells you the truth.
words.fill_blank: Her ______ apology moved him to forgive her.

words.meaning_en: To understand and share the feelings of another person
words.example_sentence: A good friend can empathize with your pain.
words.fill_blank: It is important to ______ with friends during hard times.

words.meaning_en: Comfort received by someone after a loss or disappointment
words.example_sentence: Her words of consolation helped him feel better.
words.fill_blank: Friends offer ______ when life is difficult.

words.meaning_en: A close and harmonious relationship where people understand each other
words.example_sentence: The teacher quickly built a good rapport with the students.
words.fill_blank: Building ______ takes time, honesty, and effort.

words.meaning_en: A failure to understand something correctly; a disagreement
words.example_sentence: Many friendships break because of a small misunderstanding.
words.fill_blank: A simple conversation could have cleared the ______.

words.meaning_en: Unity and support among a group with a common interest
words.example_sentence: The students showed solidarity by supporting the expelled classmate.
words.fill_blank: Standing in ______ with a friend shows real loyalty.

words.meaning_en: Pleasant and likeable; suited to one's nature
words.example_sentence: He found a congenial group of friends who shared his interests.
words.fill_blank: She felt at ease in the ______ company of her classmates.

words.meaning_en: Casual conversation about others, often involving rumours
words.example_sentence: Spreading gossip can damage friendships permanently.
words.fill_blank: Never ______ about a friend behind their back.

words.meaning_en: The act of forgiving someone for their wrongdoing
words.example_sentence: His genuine apology earned him her forgiveness.
words.fill_blank: A lasting friendship requires ______ and understanding.

words.meaning_en: So close that they are always together; impossible to separate
words.example_sentence: The two sisters were inseparable since childhood.
words.fill_blank: The best friends were ______ throughout their school years.

words.meaning_en: Done in a friendly spirit; without hostility
words.example_sentence: The two rivals reached an amicable settlement.
words.fill_blank: Their differences were resolved in an ______ manner.

words.meaning_en: Mutual trust and friendship among a group of people
words.example_sentence: The cricket team's camaraderie was obvious on and off the field.
words.fill_blank: Years of working together built a strong sense of ______.

words.meaning_en: Enjoying the company of other people; sociable
words.example_sentence: His gregarious personality made him popular with everyone.
words.fill_blank: A ______ person finds it easy to make new friends.

words.meaning_en: Experienced or done by each of two or more parties
words.example_sentence: Their friendship was based on mutual respect and honesty.
words.fill_blank: Strong friendships are built on ______ trust.

words.meaning_en: The state of being no longer close or friendly
words.example_sentence: Years of estrangement made it hard for them to reconnect.
words.fill_blank: A small argument led to a long ______ between the two families.

words.hindi: विश्वास में बात बताना / राज़ बताना
words.meaning_en: To tell someone a secret or private matter in trust
words.example_sentence: She confided in her best friend about her family problems.
words.fill_blank: He chose to ______ in her because she was trustworthy.

words.meaning_en: The quality of being kind and giving freely to others
words.example_sentence: His generosity towards his friends was well known.
words.fill_blank: Acts of ______ strengthen the bonds of friendship.

words.meaning_en: A natural liking or sympathy for someone; a close connection
words.example_sentence: She felt an immediate affinity with her new classmate.
words.fill_blank: Their shared love of music created a strong ______.

words.meaning_en: A serious break in friendly relations
words.example_sentence: A misunderstanding caused a rift between the two childhood friends.
words.fill_blank: Jealousy created a ______ in their ten-year friendship.

words.meaning_en: Trustworthy and reliable in fulfilling promises
words.example_sentence: You need a dependable friend when life gets tough.
words.fill_blank: A ______ friend never lets you down when it matters most.

words.meaning_en: A sentimental longing for the past, especially happy memories
words.example_sentence: The reunion filled her with nostalgia for school days.
words.fill_blank: Looking at old photographs always brings on a sense of ______.

words.meaning_en: Behaving in a friendly way that makes someone feel at home
words.example_sentence: Her welcoming nature made every newcomer feel comfortable.
words.fill_blank: The ______ attitude of the group helped new members settle in.

words.meaning_en: A state of active hostility and opposition between people
words.example_sentence: Old enmity between the two families divided the village.
words.fill_blank: Years of ______ between the rivals came to an end.

words.meaning_en: Strongly influenced by emotions; overly nostalgic or romantic
words.example_sentence: She kept all the letters from her childhood friend for sentimental reasons.
words.fill_blank: He was too ______ to throw away the old greeting cards.

words.meaning_en: To regard with respect, pleasure, or approval
words.example_sentence: She admired her friend's patience and dedication.
words.fill_blank: True friends ______ each other's strengths.

words.meaning_en: Providing encouragement or emotional help
words.example_sentence: His supportive friends helped him through the difficult period.
words.fill_blank: A ______ friend believes in you even when you doubt yourself.

words.meaning_en: Having the ability to say difficult things without causing offence
words.example_sentence: A tactful friend points out your mistakes with kindness.
words.fill_blank: Be ______ when sharing honest feedback with a friend.

words.meaning_en: A gathering of people who have been apart for some time
words.example_sentence: The school reunion brought old friends together after ten years.
words.fill_blank: Everyone looked forward to the annual class ______.

words.meaning_en: Very loving and loyal; giving all of one's attention to someone
words.example_sentence: She was a devoted friend who always prioritised others.
words.fill_blank: A ______ friend sacrifices their comfort for yours.

words.meaning_en: To face or deal with a problem or difficult person directly
words.example_sentence: It is better to confront a misunderstanding than let it fester.
words.fill_blank: She decided to ______ her friend about the hurtful comment.

words.meaning_en: Loyalty and commitment to a person or group
words.example_sentence: His allegiance to his childhood friends never wavered.
words.fill_blank: True friendship is built on mutual ______.

words.meaning_en: An expression of sympathy for someone experiencing grief
words.example_sentence: He sent his condolences to the family of his friend who passed away.
words.fill_blank: Friends offer ______ when someone loses a loved one.

words.meaning_en: Gentle and kind; not harmful
words.example_sentence: Her benign teasing showed she meant no harm.
words.fill_blank: His ______ humour kept the group cheerful.

words.meaning_en: To respond to a gesture or feeling in kind
words.example_sentence: When she helped him, he was happy to reciprocate.
words.fill_blank: A good friend is always ready to ______ kindness.

words.meaning_en: Polite and respectful in manner
words.example_sentence: He was always courteous to both friends and strangers.
words.fill_blank: A ______ friend makes others feel valued and respected.

words.hindi: दुख में शामिल होना / सहानुभूति देना
words.meaning_en: To express or feel sympathy with someone in their misfortune
words.example_sentence: They sat together to commiserate after losing the match.
words.fill_blank: True friends ______ with you in failure and celebrate with you in success.

words.meaning_en: The quality of being open and honest in speech
words.example_sentence: She appreciated her friend's candour even when it was hard to hear.
words.fill_blank: Friends who speak with ______ strengthen the relationship.

words.meaning_en: Held dear and valued greatly
words.example_sentence: Old friendships are among the most cherished parts of life.
words.fill_blank: That letter remains one of her most ______ possessions.

words.meaning_en: Not excluding any member of a group; welcoming to all
words.example_sentence: An inclusive friend never makes others feel left out.
words.fill_blank: The group was ______ and welcomed students from all backgrounds.

words.meaning_en: Not subject to any conditions; absolute and total
words.example_sentence: A mother's love and a true friend's support are unconditional.
words.fill_blank: The best friendships are built on ______ trust.

words.meaning_en: A close friendship without romantic involvement
words.example_sentence: Their platonic friendship lasted for over twenty years.
words.fill_blank: They shared a deep ______ bond with no romantic feelings.

words.meaning_en: Faithfulness and loyalty to a person, cause, or belief
words.example_sentence: Her fidelity to her friends was never in question.
words.fill_blank: ______ and honesty are the pillars of any deep friendship.

words.meaning_en: The process by which plants make food using sunlight, water, and carbon dioxide
words.example_sentence: Without photosynthesis, plants would not be able to produce food.
words.fill_blank: Plants use ______ to convert sunlight into food.

words.meaning_en: The movement of water through a semi-permeable membrane from a dilute to a concentrated solution
words.example_sentence: Water enters plant roots by the process of osmosis.
words.fill_blank: Salt draws water out of cells through the process of ______.

words.meaning_en: The bending of light as it passes from one medium into another
words.example_sentence: A straw in a glass of water appears bent due to refraction.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of light through a prism creates a rainbow of colours.

words.meaning_en: The speed of an object in a given direction
words.example_sentence: The velocity of the car increased as it went downhill.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of sound is approximately 343 metres per second in air.

words.meaning_en: A thread-like structure inside a cell nucleus that carries genetic information
words.example_sentence: Humans have 46 chromosomes arranged in 23 pairs.
words.fill_blank: The sex of a child is determined by the ______ inherited from the father.

words.meaning_en: The chemical process of burning in which a substance reacts with oxygen to produce heat and light
words.example_sentence: The combustion of coal in power plants produces carbon dioxide.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of fossil fuels releases carbon dioxide into the atmosphere.

words.meaning_en: The tendency of an object to remain at rest or in motion unless acted on by an external force
words.example_sentence: Due to inertia, passengers jerk forward when a bus brakes suddenly.
words.fill_blank: Newton's first law of motion is also called the law of ______.

words.meaning_en: The passing of physical and mental traits from parents to offspring through genes
words.example_sentence: The colour of a child's eyes is largely determined by heredity.
words.fill_blank: Gregor Mendel is known as the father of ______.

words.meaning_en: The body's ability to resist or fight off infection and disease
words.example_sentence: Vaccines strengthen the body's immunity against dangerous diseases.
words.fill_blank: A healthy diet and exercise help build natural ______ against illness.

words.meaning_en: A bacterium, virus, or other microorganism that causes disease
words.example_sentence: A pathogen enters the body and triggers an immune response.
words.fill_blank: Malaria is caused by a ______ transmitted through the bite of an infected mosquito.

words.meaning_en: The identification of an illness or condition through examination and tests
words.example_sentence: An early diagnosis of cancer greatly improves the chances of recovery.
words.fill_blank: The doctor made a ______ of pneumonia after examining the patient.

words.meaning_en: A disease that can spread easily from one person to another by contact
words.example_sentence: Influenza is contagious and can spread through coughing and sneezing.
words.fill_blank: Chickenpox is a ______ disease that spreads rapidly among children.

words.meaning_en: The practice of one country taking political and economic control over another
words.example_sentence: India suffered under British colonialism for nearly two centuries.
words.fill_blank: The freedom movement was a direct response to the ______ of the British Empire.

words.meaning_en: A policy of extending a country's power and influence through military force or diplomacy
words.example_sentence: European imperialism led to the colonisation of Asia and Africa.
words.fill_blank: The scramble for Africa was driven by European ______.

words.meaning_en: A strong feeling of pride and loyalty towards one's country, often driving movements for independence
words.example_sentence: The spirit of nationalism united Indians against British rule.
words.fill_blank: The rise of ______ in the 19th century led to the unification of Germany and Italy.

words.meaning_en: A fundamental and rapid change in a society or government, often achieved through force
words.example_sentence: The French Revolution changed the political landscape of Europe.
words.fill_blank: The Industrial ______ transformed manufacturing and changed people's way of life.

words.meaning_en: A set of fundamental laws that establishes the principles of a government and the rights of citizens
words.example_sentence: India adopted its Constitution on January 26, 1950.
words.fill_blank: Dr B.R. Ambedkar was the principal architect of the Indian ______.

words.meaning_en: The highest legislative body of a country that debates and passes laws
words.example_sentence: A bill must be passed in Parliament before it becomes a law.
words.fill_blank: The Indian ______ consists of the Lok Sabha and the Rajya Sabha.

words.meaning_en: A medieval social system where lords owned land and peasants worked it in exchange for protection
words.example_sentence: Under feudalism, peasants had very few rights and freedoms.
words.fill_blank: The decline of ______ in Europe gave rise to new social and economic classes.

words.meaning_en: The full right and supreme power of a state to govern itself without outside interference
words.example_sentence: After independence, India regained full sovereignty over its land.
words.fill_blank: A nation's ______ means it has the right to make its own laws and policies.

words.meaning_en: A union or formal agreement between countries or groups for mutual benefit
words.example_sentence: The two nations formed an alliance to fight against a common enemy.
words.fill_blank: The Allied Powers formed an ______ to oppose Nazi Germany in World War II.

words.meaning_en: A triangular landform created by sediment deposited where a river meets the sea
words.example_sentence: The Sundarbans is the world's largest river delta.
words.fill_blank: The Ganga-Brahmaputra ______ is one of the most fertile regions in the world.

words.meaning_en: A piece of land almost entirely surrounded by water but connected to a larger landmass
words.example_sentence: India is a peninsula as it is surrounded by sea on three sides.
words.fill_blank: The Deccan Plateau forms much of the Indian ______.

words.meaning_en: The angular distance of a place north or south of the Earth's equator, measured in degrees
words.example_sentence: New Delhi is located at approximately 28° north latitude.
words.fill_blank: The Tropic of Cancer lies at 23.5° north ______.

words.meaning_en: A partially enclosed coastal area where a river meets the sea and freshwater mixes with saltwater
words.example_sentence: Many migratory birds nest in the estuary during winter months.
words.fill_blank: The ______ is an important habitat for fish and water birds.

words.meaning_en: The seasonal reversal of wind direction that brings heavy rainfall to South Asia
words.example_sentence: Farmers in India depend on the monsoon for irrigating their crops.
words.fill_blank: The southwest ______ arrives in India around June every year.

words.meaning_en: A river or stream that flows into and joins a larger river
words.example_sentence: The Yamuna is a major tributary of the Ganga.
words.fill_blank: The Chambal is a ______ of the Yamuna river.

words.meaning_en: A general rise in prices over time that reduces the purchasing power of money
words.example_sentence: High inflation means people can buy fewer goods with the same amount of money.
words.fill_blank: The government raises interest rates to control ______.

words.meaning_en: The income received by a government from taxes or by a business from its activities
words.example_sentence: The government uses tax revenue to build roads and schools.
words.fill_blank: The state earns ______ from taxes on goods and services.

words.meaning_en: The desire and ability of consumers to purchase goods or services at a given price
words.example_sentence: When the demand for a product rises, its price usually increases.
words.fill_blank: The law of ______ states that as price rises, quantity demanded falls.

words.meaning_en: The system by which a government collects compulsory payments from citizens to fund public services
words.example_sentence: Progressive taxation means richer citizens pay a higher percentage of their income.
words.fill_blank: Direct ______ includes income tax paid directly to the government.

words.meaning_en: A system of exchange where goods or services are traded directly without the use of money
words.example_sentence: Before money was invented, people used barter to trade goods.
words.fill_blank: In the ______ system, a farmer might exchange grain for tools.

words.meaning_en: A figure of speech in which one thing is described as being something else to suggest a resemblance
words.example_sentence: The phrase 'life is a journey' is a metaphor comparing life to travel.
words.fill_blank: The poet used a ______ to describe time as a thief.

words.meaning_en: A story in which characters and events represent abstract ideas or moral lessons
words.example_sentence: George Orwell's Animal Farm is an allegory of the Russian Revolution.
words.fill_blank: Plato's cave is a famous ______ about the nature of knowledge and reality.

words.meaning_en: A group of lines forming a unit in a poem, similar to a paragraph in prose
words.example_sentence: The poem had five stanzas, each exploring a different emotion.
words.fill_blank: Each ______ of the poem ended with a rhyming couplet.

words.meaning_en: A spoken or written account of a series of connected events; a story
words.example_sentence: The narrative of the novel was told from the child's perspective.
words.fill_blank: The first-person ______ makes the story feel personal and immediate.

words.meaning_en: The use of words to convey the opposite of their literal meaning, or a situation opposite to what is expected
words.example_sentence: It was an irony that the fire station caught fire.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the story was that the detective was himself the criminal.

words.meaning_en: The main character in a story, novel, film, or play
words.example_sentence: The protagonist of the novel faces great hardship before achieving his goal.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the story overcomes many hardships to reach her goal.

words.meaning_en: The use of symbols to represent ideas, qualities, or concepts beyond their literal meaning
words.example_sentence: In the poem, the dove is used as symbolism for peace.
words.fill_blank: The green light in The Great Gatsby is considered ______ for the American Dream.

words.meaning_en: The variety of plant and animal life in a particular habitat or on Earth as a whole
words.example_sentence: Deforestation threatens the biodiversity of tropical rainforests.
words.fill_blank: Protecting ______ is essential to maintaining healthy ecosystems.

words.meaning_en: A resource that is naturally replenished and can be used repeatedly without being exhausted
words.example_sentence: Solar and wind energy are renewable sources of power.
words.fill_blank: India is investing heavily in ______ energy to reduce dependence on coal.

words.meaning_en: The production and discharge of something, especially greenhouse gases into the atmosphere
words.example_sentence: Reducing carbon emissions is essential to slow global warming.
words.fill_blank: Factories and vehicles are major sources of carbon ______.

words.meaning_en: A reduction in the quantity or richness of something valuable over time
words.example_sentence: The depletion of the ozone layer has increased harmful UV radiation reaching Earth.
words.fill_blank: Overuse of groundwater has led to the rapid ______ of water reserves.

words.meaning_en: The process of making something impure or harmful by introducing a pollutant
words.example_sentence: Industrial waste causes contamination of rivers and groundwater.
words.fill_blank: The ______ of the drinking water supply led to a disease outbreak.

words.meaning_en: The unjust or prejudicial treatment of people based on race, gender, religion, or other characteristics
words.example_sentence: The civil rights movement fought against racial discrimination in America.
words.fill_blank: Caste-based ______ is prohibited under the Indian Constitution.

words.meaning_en: Prolonged and cruel exercise of authority or power that keeps people in subjugation
words.example_sentence: The workers rose against the oppression of the factory owners.
words.fill_blank: Gandhi used non-violent resistance to fight colonial ______.

words.meaning_en: To give someone the authority, confidence, or means to act independently
words.example_sentence: Education can empower women to make independent decisions.
words.fill_blank: Government schemes aim to ______ rural communities through skill development.

words.meaning_en: A change or improvement made to a law, social system, or institution
words.example_sentence: Land reform redistributed farmland to poor farmers across the country.
words.fill_blank: The abolition of child marriage was a major social ______ in India.

words.meaning_en: The right to vote in public and political elections
words.example_sentence: Women fought for suffrage to gain the right to vote in the early 20th century.
words.fill_blank: Universal adult ______ means every citizen above 18 can vote.

words.meaning_en: Having the ability to read and write; educated and knowledgeable
words.example_sentence: A literate population is essential for the development of a democratic nation.
words.fill_blank: India's goal is to make every citizen ______ by improving access to education.
